headline: The S&P 500’s 1.5% Selloff Is Driven By A Few Key Factors
summary: The S&P 500 closed at 666.06 on Thursday, falling 1.52% as Iranian strikes on two oil tankers sent crude prices surging toward $100 a barrel, reigniting inflation fears and triggering a broad selloff that spared almost no sector except energy. Reuters described it as the S&P 500’s biggest three-day percentage drop in a month, with ... The S&P 500’s 1.5% Selloff Is Driven By A Few Key Factors

headline: Sandisk Surges on AI Storage Demand: Is the Rally Already Priced in?
summary: SNDK stock has soared over 1,100% in a year as AI-driven NAND demand lifts its outlook, but a rich valuation and high sales multiple are raising timing concerns.

headline: 3 Nasdaq 100 Stocks to Research Further
summary: The Nasdaq 100 (^NDX) is packed with high-growth companies, and while the market is competitive, some are pulling ahead. A handful of standout businesses are continuing to scale, delivering strong financials and market leadership.

headline: Cramer: Buy Western Digital, Micron, Seagate on oil-driven dips

headline: Dow Jones Futures Fall; Crude Oil Prices Top $90 On Tanker Attacks, Port Disruption
summary: Futures fell as oil prices kept rising amid tanker attacks and port disruptions amid the Iran war. Nvidia, Oracle news lifted AI stocks on Wednesday.

headline: Iran War Unlikely to Hurt AI Supply Chain for Now, Seagate Says
summary: Both have warned their hard drives are sold out for 2026.  “At this point, we have resiliency in our supply chain that is buffering us from any short-term impact,” Seagate Chief Commercial Officer Ban Seng Teh said on Bloomberg TV Thursday, adding that Seagate is exploring “several other options,” to shore up its supplies.
